channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US expects hostages freed on Monday as Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Gaza ceasefire held in its second day as tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians returned to their neighborhoods Saturday and Israelis cheered Monday's expected release of remaining hostages. “Gaza is completely destroyed.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, SAM METZ, SARAH EL DEEB and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jared Kushner reenters as key player in Middle East peace push
item.description: Jared Kushner has reentered the political spotlight as a top envoy for the Trump White House in efforts to secure a peace deal in the Middle East, highlighting both his influence in the region and his growing business portfolio there.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump peace plan for Gaza could be just a 'pause' before Hamas strikes again, experts warn
item.description: Regional coordination supports President Donald Trump's Gaza peace plan, but analysts caution that Hamas's continued existence could reignite conflict.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel, amid Gaza ceasefire, kills at least 1 in overnight strikes on Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military launched airstrikes on Lebanon overnight targeting Hezbollah and killing at least one person. The attack on the village of Msayleh also destroyed a highway that connects Lebanon to Beirut, the Lebanese Health Ministry said, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago leaders, residents share mixed emotions and cautious hope after Israel-Hamas ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Trump to visit Egypt and Israel early next week amid ceasefire deal in Middle East
item.description: President Trump said Friday he plans to visit Egypt and Israel during a one-day trip to the Middle East early next week to mark the implementation of a peace agreement that will see a pause in fighting in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday for her struggle to achieve a democratic transition in the South American nation, winning recognition as a woman “who keeps the flame of democracy burning amid a growing darkness.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, REGINA GARCIA CANO and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, The Hill

item.title: Melania Trump in talks with Putin on reuniting Ukrainian children with families
item.description: First lady Melania Trump said Friday she has been in communication with Russian President Vladimir Putin about reuniting Ukrainian children displaced by war with their families.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: The Taliban government blames Pakistan for a strike on Kabul
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The Taliban government on Friday blamed Pakistan for striking the Afghan capital and bombing a market in the country's east, accusing its neighbor of violating national airspace. A blast struck before 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel-Hamas ceasefire takes effect, IDF begins pulling back in Gaza
item.description: Israeli troops began repositioning to pre-determined locations as the ceasefire with Hamas began, bringing the hostages one step closer to going home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO begins a big nuclear exercise next week. Protecting the weapons is a key part of the drill
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO will hold its major annual nuclear exercise next week, the alliance’s chief announced Friday, with an important part of the drill to focus on protecting the weapons before they're ever used.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: Philippine seismologists say that a new offshore earthquake with a preliminary 6.8 magnitude hit the same southern region struck earlier by an earthquake with a magnitude of 7.4.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Israel and Hamas agree to a ceasefire, release of hostages and prisoners
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump expects Israeli hostages to be released on Monday
item.description: President Donald Trump says he expects the remaining hostages to be released from Gaza on Monday as part of the first phase of Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Peace celebrations in Gaza marred by daunting rebuilding phase to come
item.description: A freelance photographer based in Gaza details his hope for life in the war-torn city now that a ceasefire deal has been reached.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: US diplomat fired over relationship with woman accused of Chinese Communist Party ties
item.description: The dismissal is believed to be the first of its kind for violating a ban on such relationships that was introduced late last year under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas accepts Trump peace plan ending 2 years of war in Gaza, returning hostages
item.description: Hamas agrees to President Donald Trump's Gaza peace plan that calls for release of all 48 hostages and complete Israeli withdrawal in exchange for Hamas disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Before and after images show devastating destruction in Gaza
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals widespread devastation in Gaza cities, including Rafah and Beit Hanoun, since the start of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strike seriously damages Ukrainian power plant as winter approaches, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia seriously damaged one of Ukraine’s thermal power plants in an overnight attack, authorities said Wednesday, as Moscow pursued its campaign to deny Ukrainians heat, light and running water as winter approaches.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli military intercepts another flotilla heading to Gaza, detains scores of activists
item.description: The ministry said the 145 activists, who were in good health, were being brought to shore in Israel for processing and are expected to be deported soon.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza peace talks appear to gain momentum on the war's anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Peace talks between Israel and Hamas resumed at an Egyptian resort city on Tuesday, the two-year anniversary of the militant group's surprise attack on Israel that started the war that has killed tens of thousands of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu, on 2-year mark of Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack, says Israel 'not broken,' vows to bring hostages home
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu marked two years since Hamas’ Oct. 7 assault in a statement issued on X, honoring the victims and vowing Israel’s war for survival continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greta Thunberg is among flotilla activists deported from Israel. Others remain in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli authorities said Monday they deported to Greece and Slovakia another 171 people detained for taking part in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla, including prominent Swedish activist Greta Thunberg.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Snowstorm traps hundreds of hikers on Mount Everest during China's national holiday
item.description: About 350 hikers had reached a meeting point in Tingri country and rescuers were in contact with another 200, state broadcaster CCTV said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says it struck Russian ammunition plant, oil terminal and weapons depot
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Long-range Ukrainian drones and missiles hit a major Russian ammunition plant, a key oil terminal and an important weapons depot behind the front line, Ukraine's president and military said Monday, as Kyiv cranked up pressure on Moscow’s military logistics.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Negotiators meet in Egypt seeking Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Talks are split into phases: first, securing the release of hostages, then tackling Hamas' demilitarization.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Hamas prepare for negotiations in Egypt ahead of possible Gaza ceasefire
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel and Hamas prepared for indirect negotiations in Egypt on Monday, as hopes for a possible ceasefire in Gaza grew after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said a hostage release could be announced this week. Tuesday marks two years since the Hamas attack that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9
